items.headline: Clarity and Congress's summer break: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: All parties are still optimistic that Clarity can happen before the midterms, but time is really starting to run out.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Luxembourg upgrades Ripple's preliminary crypto asset provider to fully compliant
items.subheadline: The approval means Ripple is now fully MiCA-compliant for payments, financial institutions, corporates and businesses across all 30 European Economic Area countries.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Bitcoin's U.S. reserve still a work-in-progress as federal agencies hash it out
items.subheadline: The White House says it's still evaluating the "best structure" for the federal fund to hold bitcoin as a long-term reserve and a separate stockpile of other crypto assets.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Tokenized securities need competition, not gatekeepers
items.subheadline: Innovation thrives when investors have choices. For tokenized securities, Washington shouldn't pick winners before the market has a chance to learn what works, argues Patrick McHenry, vice chairman at Ondo Finance and former Chairman of the House Financial Services Committee.
items.author: Patrick McHenry

items.headline: Europe is closing the door on offshore crypto, but it’s leaving the riskiest window open
items.subheadline: MiCA was never meant to address the giant crypto derivatives market. That could pose a serious problem, says Patrick Gruhn, founder and chief executive of Perpetuals.com.
items.author: Patrick Gruhn

items.headline: Europe led on crypto regulation. Now implementation must match ambition
items.author: Richard Teng

items.headline: Collateral, not yield, will decide which stablecoins win
items.subheadline: As yield-bearing stablecoins race toward a $50 billion market capitalization, the industry is optimizing for the wrong metric, argues Artem Tolkachev, chief RWA officer at Falcon Finance.
items.author: Artem Tolkachev

items.headline: XRP price news: Why rally in  Ripple-linked token stalled near $1.15
items.subheadline: Buyers defended the session lows and pushed XRP back toward resistance, but muted overall volume left traders waiting for confirmation above $1.13-$1.14.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: BONK faces $20 million treasury drain after attacker spends $4 million to pass malicious proposal
items.subheadline: The attacker bought enough of the memecoin's tokens to pass a governance proposal that sent the group's holdings to a wallet they controlled, then began selling.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: FBI Director Kash Patel caught sleeping on required disclosure of six-figure MSTR investment
items.subheadline: Patel loaded up on BTC-holder MSTR shares but failed to file a timely disclosure, according to a report by nonpartisan news outlet NOTUS.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: The quantum clock is ticking: it's Bitcoin's problem, not Ethereum's
items.subheadline: A recent research note published by Citi analysts reached a conclusion about quantum risk that should give every institutional bitcoin holder pause, Tabar explains.
items.author: Samir Tabar

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: A new nonprofit launches with a focus on Wall Street and institutional adoption
items.subheadline: The launch comes as the Ethereum Foundation narrows its focus to stewarding the core protocol, with independent organizations like EthLabs emerging to take on ecosystem functions such as research & development.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: EthLabs launches as Ethereum undergoes its biggest leadership transition in years
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re diving into the creation of EthLabs, and why it was launched during a period of transition for the ecosystem.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: A plan to freeze the creator's Bitcoin sparks fierce debate over crypto rules
items.subheadline: Industry leaders warn that locking up Satoshi Nakamoto's wallet crosses a dangerous line and goes against the foundational ethos of digital assets.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Tokenization's next use case is personalized portfolios, NYLIM executive says
items.subheadline: Thomas Sy, head of multi-asset solutions at the $800-billion asset manager, says blockchain can enable complex portfolio construction that's not yet possible in traditional finance.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: How big banks plan to capture a quadrillion-dollar market
items.subheadline: Financial institutions are racing to become the secure gateways for existing stablecoins as digital asset volume is projected to explode by 2030.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Coinbase AI alert draws backlash after pushing World Cup result before kickoff
items.subheadline: CEO Brian Armstrong investigated, and the firm confirmed they made updates to prevent future AI-generated inaccuracies.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Russia's largest bank plans crypto wallet launch as Moscow clears market path
items.subheadline: Services will integrate into "Sberbank Online" and "SberInvestments" once the "On Digital Currency and Digital Rights" bill takes effect in September.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Ethereum news: Bitmine (BMNR) buys 42k ETH while Strategy sells bitcoin (BTC)
items.subheadline: The second-largest digital asset treasury increased its buying pace, while Strategy sold a chunk of its bitcoin holdings.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Securitize (SECZ) eyes acquisitions with $400 million war chest after going public
items.subheadline: Following its NYSE debut, Securitize CEO Carlos Domingo said the firm wants to expand its institutional tokenization platform rather than buy competitors.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Stablecoin trading volume is on track to smash records in 2026
items.subheadline: As Wall Street banks adopt digital currencies for faster settlements, overall trading volume spiked 63% in just a single month.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Ethereum Institutional wins backing from Standard Chartered and top Ethereum leaders
items.subheadline: Its launch comes as Ethereum's support ecosystem undergoes a broader evolution, following the debut of EthLabs and amid ongoing efforts by the Ethereum Foundation to respond to community criticism over transparency, communication and its role within the ecosystem.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin rises above $61,000 as U.S. jobs data for June disappoints
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Live updates: More bitcoin is now held at a loss than at a profit
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: How white hat hackers with a $3,000 server found a flaw that could've put $70 billion in crypto at risk
items.subheadline: A critical flaw in the Aptos blockchain gave researchers a near-90% success rate at breaking a core security guarantee, with attack costs of just hundreds of dollars.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Americans traded $571 million on Polymarket politic bets despite U.S. ban
items.subheadline: U.S.-linked wallets traded $571 million in political contracts over the past year, more than any other country, even though the platform legally cannot serve them. The money leans toward the foreign-conflict markets U.S. venues do not list.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin slips after Strategy sells 3,588 BTC for $216 million
items.subheadline: Strategy raised $216 milliion with the sale of 3,588 bitcoin last week, sending prices lower earlier on Monday.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Vitalik Buterin calls lean Ethereum its biggest rebuild since the Merge
items.subheadline: A revised roadmap details how the multi-year overhaul would replace nearly every major part of Ethereum's protocol, with quantum resistance and privacy moving up the priority list. It lands as ether rallies more than 12% in the past seven days.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) developers embrace Vitalik Buterin's long-term vision but urge quicker execution
items.subheadline: The "Lean Ethereum" strawmap, updated earlier this week, has gained support from many of the network's researchers, though several say the biggest challenge won't be deciding what to build, but how quickly it can happen.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: The U.S. government is betting $2 Billion on quantum computing, and the defense side can't keep up
items.subheadline: Pruden argues that to defend against a quantum computer capable of cryptographically relevant operations, we need post-quantum cryptography and regulatory coordination that the industry has been deferring for years.
items.author: Alex  Pruden

items.headline: Stablecoins Were Meant to Disrupt Finance. Instead, They Became Idle Cash.
items.subheadline: O’Connor argues that crypto’s clearest success story has scaled as money but not as capital.
items.author: John O’Connor

items.headline: If America wants to lead in crypto, it must protect the people who build it
items.subheadline: Despite the Clarity Act’s advancement toward the finish line, there’s one provision under threat for builders that can’t be overlooked, argues Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
items.subheadline: Without an update to the current auditing infrastructure, the crypto space will likely continue to suffer significant losses, explains Beyer.
items.author: Stefan Beyer

items.headline: The SEC delayed tokenizing stocks, and here’s why that’s a relief
items.subheadline: Before we rush to tokenize U.S. equities, we need compliance systems that understand context, not just checkboxes. Investors like Michael Burry see the danger, so should the rest of us, urges Chamarajnagar.
items.author: Dr. Ravishankar Chamarajnagar

items.headline: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
items.subheadline: Congress should not kneecap one of the clearest advances in payment infrastructure to protect community banks from a threat that has not been proven, argues Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.
items.author: Ryne Sax

items.headline: What Robinhood’s recent layoffs say about the current state of crypto investments
items.subheadline: Despite the trading platform’s restructuring and the wave of crypto companies making cuts, signaling a late bear market, Altcoin Pro’s Horst, Anderson and Zhuleku explain there’s no reason to panic.
items.author: Ryan Horst

items.headline: ChatGPT developer OpenAI reported to discuss offering U.S. government a 5% stake
items.subheadline: Proposal aims to give the public a financial stake in AI while easing political scrutiny of the industry.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: MiCA became law 3 years ago, now Europe's crypto framework is undergoing a rethink
items.subheadline: Europe’s MiCA regime is now up for review, known colloquially as “MiCA 2.0,” through a consultation that closes around September.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: US Treasury sanctions over 100 ISIS-K crypto addresses in latest enforcement action
items.subheadline: ISIS-K allegedly used its media wing to solicit donations via Tron, Monero, and Bitcoin, highlighting stablecoin issuers' growing role in sanctions enforcement.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Binance pushes back on reports that EU regulators tried to block it
items.subheadline: The exchange denies claims that its financial-crime compliance fell short, calling allegations that it ignored major sanctions concerns categorically false.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Donald Trump says there's 'nothing wrong' with his $1.4 billion crypto windfall
items.subheadline: The president reported at least $1.4 billion in crypto income for 2025. He told CNBC he did nothing illegal and was not aware of the extent of his holdings. Critics say he is profiting from the office while his administration writes the industry's rules.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: UK's bold new crypto rules promise to unlock global trading, but huge compliance hurdles still threaten the rollout
items.subheadline: The FCA's crypto framework has been praised for preserving global liquidity and supporting tokenization but firms will face significant hurdles to approval.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: A massive EU regulatory crackdown is threatening the explosive boom of multibillion-dollar prediction markets
items.subheadline: Regulators emphasized that a product's actual function as a derivative matters more than its commercial name or labeling when assessing compliance.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Live markets: Bitcoin and ether ETFs drew inflows Monday
items.subheadline: Bitcoin and ether ETFs drew fresh inflows Monday as the bid starts to return.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin's (BTC) recent macro relief faces a challenge from Japanese interest rates
items.subheadline: Japanese bond yields continue to rise, lifting their U.S. counterparts posing a potential headwind for risk assets.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Bitcoin holders can now participate in a BTC covered call income strategy on Binance
items.subheadline: The product, called BTC Yield, is designed exclusively for people who already hold bitcoin.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Bitcoin pulls back from $64,500 as weak ETF flows, falling open interest cloud outlook
items.subheadline: BTC retreated from a two-week high of $64,500 as falling open interest and weak spot demand cast doubt on the sustainability of July's 8.4% market recovery.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: BTC price July rise at risk as Coinbase Premium logs 50-day negative streak: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: The day ahead in crypto: July 7, 2026
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Coinbase secures UK authorization to offer traditional investments alongside crypto
items.subheadline: The license allows institutional traders to access perpetual futures and gives UK retail customers the ability to trade equities to begin with.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Bitcoin, XRP draw Japanese firms as weak yen drives treasury diversification
items.subheadline: SBI VC Trade says corporate demand for crypto is rising as a weak yen pushes firms to diversify reserves, part of a run that took its registered accounts past 2 million.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ex-Tether CIO plans to sell a piece of his stake in the crypto giant
items.subheadline: Richard Heathcote is reportedly working with PJT Partners to offload a fraction of his 1.26% holding following his shift to an advisory role.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: EDX Markets raises $76 million in Series C funding round led by SBI Holdings
items.subheadline: EDX’s model targets institutional clients by separating trading from custody via a central clearinghouse to minimize counterparty risk.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: AI trade loses steam as Samsung earnings fail to lift chip stocks amid open source AI shift
items.subheadline: Record earnings, rising competition and concerns over slowing AI spending are forcing investors to reassess one of the market's biggest trades.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Vanguard opens search for digital assets leader in sign of evolving crypto strategy
items.subheadline: The new role would oversee tokenization, stablecoins and blockchain initiatives as Vanguard reassesses digital assets.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Analysts see more upside for SpaceX as post-IPO research begins
items.subheadline: Major brokerages initiated coverage on Tuesday with mostly buy ratings following the company's $75 billion June IPO.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: U.S. SEC to propose crypto rule as soon as this month to ease startups, fundraising
items.subheadline: A newly updated agenda from the securities regulator shows so-called Reg Crypto high on its near-term list.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: SpaceX IPO powers record $3.86 billion in tokenized equities trading in June
items.subheadline: SpaceX tokens captured $1.19 billion, or 31% of total monthly volume, with Backpack’s SPCX leading trading at $1.08 billion.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Crypto exchange Kraken is trying to become a bank in Europe
items.subheadline: The focus is on Lithuania as the jurisdiction to secure the license, according to a person familiar with the plans.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: Securitize (SECZ), BlackRock's tokenization partner, slides 40% after SPAC debut
items.subheadline: The decline fits into a pattern of recently-public digital asset companies sliding after debut, Arca's Jeff Dorman said.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: BTC, XRP, ETH news: Major tokens under pressure as U.S. attacks Iran
items.subheadline: Major cryptocurrencies are trading in the red as renewed U.S.-Iran airstrikes push oil higher.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Live updates: Japan's collapsing yen is pushing companies into bitcoin and XRP
items.subheadline: Hedge funds have turned the most bearish on the yen since 2007, boosting bets on further losses to nearly 138,000 contracts as of June 30.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa
